calendar.matters: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets

Petition By Administrator For Instructions
Off Calendar May-26-2026 Continued To Jun-23-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition By Administrator For Instructions
Off Calendar Feb-17-2026 Continued To May-26-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles Drexler on behalf of Petitioner Carolyn F. Strauss.
Mercy Hall, Esq. appearing for and with Bill Hall.

Court allows the administrator to file a drop off ex parte to request guardian ad litem for conservatee & waives the declaration requirement.

Court orders matter continued to May-26-2026 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca. Clerk: S. Monroe. Deputy: Musto. Not reported.

Petition By Administrator For Instructions
Off Calendar Nov-25-2025 Continued To Feb-17-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Charles Drexler for administrator Carolyn Strauss (via Zoom).
Mercy Hall for fiduciary Bill Hall, who was also present (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Petition by Administrator for Instructions.

The Court inquired into the nature of Bill Hall's authority and his role with respect to Ms. Khan. Ms. Hall stated that Mr. Hall was the named trustee of Ms. Khan's revocable living trust pursuant to estate planning documents prepared by attorney Melissa Keys, which also included powers of attorney and a pour-over will. She stated that the trust was executed on June 24, 2025 and that Ms. Keys had determined Ms. Khan possessed capacity to execute those instruments. She explained that Medi-Cal's temporary removal of the asset limit supported the use of a living trust rather than a special needs trust. She stated Mr. Hall is a licensed professional fiduciary, not a private fiduciary.

Mr. Drexler raised concerns regarding Ms. Khan's capacity based on her long-term placement in a care facility, his observations of diminished cognition, turnover of attorneys, inconsistent explanations, and lack of transparency. He also indicated he lacked information about Ms. Khan's agents under her powers of attorney.

The Court found the record insufficient to determine Ms. Khan's present capacity and stated it would require a physician's capacity declaration and may appoint an independent attorney guardian ad litem if incapacity is shown. The Court ordered that no distributions be made to any person claiming authority to act for Ms. Khan and directed that the approximately $300,000 held in Mr. Drexler's attorney-client trust account remain there pending further order.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to February 17, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. The Court indicated that, if Mr. Drexler obtains a capacity declaration before then, an ex parte request for appointment of a guardian ad litem should be filed.

The Court denied Mr. Drexler's request for $7,260 in extraordinary fees, granting only $454.10, and denied the remainder without prejudice to any remedies outside the probate proceeding.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
